name: UCLA Space Institute (SPACE)
type: Institute, Division of Physical Sciences
relevance: An interdisciplinary institute that coordinates space-related research across the departments of Earth, Planetary, and Space Sciences (EPSS), Atmospheric and Oceanic Sciences (AOS), and Physics and Astronomy.

name: Plasma Science and Technology Institute (PSTI)
type: Institute, Interdisciplinary
relevance: Investigates fundamental plasma science, fusion energy, and space/astrophysical plasmas through a collaboration of faculty from Engineering and Physical Sciences.

name: Robotics and Mechanisms Laboratory (RoMeLa)
type: Research Lab, Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering
relevance: A world-renowned facility for robotics research focusing on humanoid locomotion, novel mobile robots, and autonomous systems for exploration.

name: Bruin Spacecraft Group (Bruin Space)
type: Student Organization, Samueli School of Engineering
relevance: UCLA's premier student-led space organization, developing CubeSats, high-altitude balloons, and amateur rocketry payloads.

name: The Rocket Project at UCLA
type: Student Organization, Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering
relevance: Focuses on the design, construction, and launch of high-powered liquid and solid fuel rockets for collegiate competitions.

name: AIAA at UCLA (American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics)
type: Student Organization (Professional Chapter), Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering
relevance: The UCLA student chapter of the national aerospace professional society, organizing student design-build-fly teams and professional development.

name: ELFIN CubeSat Mission (Electron Losses and Fields Investigation)
type: Research Lab/Mission, Earth, Planetary, and Space Sciences
relevance: The first satellite mission fully built and operated at UCLA, utilizing CubeSats to study space weather and electron loss in the Earth's radiation belts.

name: Advanced Space Systems and Propulsion Laboratory (ASSPL)
type: Research Lab, Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering
relevance: Develops breakthrough propulsion concepts like solar sailing and plasma thrusters, alongside mission analysis and spacecraft design.

name: Energy and Propulsion Research Laboratory (EPRL)
type: Research Lab, Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering
relevance: Specializes in high-speed propulsion, combustion, and fluid mechanics for aerospace applications, including rocket and air-breathing engines.

name: Hypersonics and Computational Aerodynamics Group (HACAG)
type: Research Lab, Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering
relevance: Performs fundamental research on hypersonic flows, boundary-layer transition, and the design of hypersonic flight vehicles.

name: UCLA-AFRL Collaborative Center for Aerospace Sciences (CCAS)
type: Center, Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering / Air Force Research Laboratory
relevance: A partnership between UCLA and the Air Force Research Laboratory focusing on multi-disciplinary aerospace systems research.

name: Earth, Planetary, and Space Sciences Student Organization (EPSSSO)
type: Student Organization, Earth, Planetary, and Space Sciences
relevance: Represents students in the EPSS department, fostering community and professional development in planetary science and space physics.

name: Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS) at UCLA
type: Student Organization, Samueli School of Engineering
relevance: Designs, builds, and flies autonomous aerial vehicles for the annual AUVSI Student UAS competition.

name: Autonomous Vehicle Systems Instrumentation Laboratory (AVSIL)
type: Research Lab, Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering
relevance: Conducts research on formation flight, GPS-based navigation, and instrumentation for autonomous aerospace vehicles.

name: Sensors and Instrumentation Laboratory (SIL)
type: Research Lab, Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering
relevance: Focuses on microscale sensors, including Coriolis vibratory gyroscopes essential for aerospace navigation and robotics.

name: Basic Plasma Science Facility (BaPSF)
type: National Research Facility, Physics and Astronomy
relevance: A national collaborative facility for fundamental plasma physics research relevant to space plasmas and fusion energy.

name: Fusion Science and Technology Center (FTC)
type: Center, Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering
relevance: Focuses on the engineering and physics of fusion energy, including plasma-material interactions and magnetohydrodynamics.

name: Spinning Fluids Lab (SpinLab)
type: Research Lab, Earth, Planetary, and Space Sciences
relevance: Uses experimental fluid dynamics to study the internal processes of planets, such as core convection and magnetic field generation.

name: VECTR Lab
type: Research Lab, Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering
relevance: Specializes in autonomy algorithms and robotics, focusing on control and navigation in complex environments.

name: SCI Autonomy Lab
type: Research Lab, Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering
relevance: Researches safety-critical control and learning for autonomous systems, highly relevant to aerospace and robotics.

name: UCLA Robot Intelligence Lab (URIL)
type: Research Lab, Samueli School of Engineering
relevance: Explores robot learning, adaptation, and human-robot interaction in real-world scenarios.

name: Biomechatronics Laboratory
type: Research Lab, Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering
relevance: Develops robotic hands and tactile sensing systems, bridging the gap between biological systems and autonomous robots.

name: Bionics Laboratory
type: Research Lab, Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering
relevance: Focuses on medical robotics and surgical robots, which utilize advanced robotics and control mechanisms.

name: Institute of Geophysics and Planetary Physics (IGPP)
type: Research Institute, Earth, Planetary, and Space Sciences
relevance: A major research center for geophysics, space physics, and planetary science, hosting many of UCLA's space mission teams.

name: High Energy Density Physics (HEDP) Group / PHOENIX Laser Lab
type: Research Lab, Physics and Astronomy
relevance: Conducts experimental plasma physics research using high-energy lasers to simulate astrophysical and space phenomena.

name: Plasma Diagnostics Group (PDG)
type: Research Lab, Physics and Astronomy
relevance: Experts in advanced microwave and millimeter wave plasma diagnostics for fusion and space research.

name: Meteorite Gallery (UCLA)
type: Museum/Research Center, Earth, Planetary, and Space Sciences
relevance: Houses one of the largest meteorite collections in the world, supporting research into the formation and evolution of the solar system.

name: Infrared Laboratory
type: Research Lab, Physics and Astronomy
relevance: Develops state-of-the-art infrared instrumentation for ground-based and space-based telescopes to study the cosmos.

name: UCLA Space Physics Group
type: Research Area/Group, Earth, Planetary, and Space Sciences
relevance: Studies planetary magnetospheres, the solar wind, and the interaction between the sun and solar system objects.

name: Planetary Science Group
type: Research Area/Group, Earth, Planetary, and Space Sciences
relevance: Investigates the dynamics, physical properties, and atmospheres of planets, moons, and solar system objects.

name: UCLA MAG Lab (Space Magnetometer Lab)
type: Research Lab, Earth, Planetary, and Space Sciences
relevance: Designs and builds space-qualified magnetometers for NASA missions like THEMIS, ARTEMIS, and TRACERS.
